title: Innovative Intense Peruvian Black Beans Picarones
description: Savory and springy Peruvian picarones filled with black beans, perfect for an adventurous snack or dessert, diabetic-friendly and made with fermented dough.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the warm water and yeast, letting it sit for 5 minutes to activate.
Add the mashed sweet potato, anise seeds, pumpkin seeds, olive oil, and salt to the bowl and mix well.
Gradually add the whole wheat flour to the bowl, mixing until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it ferment in a warm place for 2 hours.
Preheat the oil in a deep frying pan to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Once the oil is hot, punch down the dough and shape it into small balls.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and fill with a tablespoon of mashed black beans.
Fold the dough over the filling and seal the edges by pressing them together with a fork.
Fry the picarones in the hot oil for 2-3 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown and puffed up.
